_risk_level_doc: high = security hook surface (Item A modifies pretool-bash-safety.sh, the authoritative bash-safety gate). A mis-converted rule can silently disable a block (false negative). Items F/C/B are SMALL-STANDARD; the cycle inherits the highest item's tier.

requirement.original: Analyze and implement 4 backlog items (F minor cleanliness, C verify+patch step-7 marker parity, B verify+harden write-guard sentinel, A extend context-stripping coverage) for the .claude/ config repo. Analysis and spec only this cycle.
requirement.clarified: Land F (commit.md:221 stale label), C (verify 3-site spec-continuation marker parity, patch gaps), B (verify write-sentinel 3-candidate parity + CF-1 overwrite-only invariant, harden gaps), A (convert ONLY the two safe rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED: dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045 command-word-anchored, and kill <PID> :1311 argument-anchored-but-kill-is-DANGER; make NO DANGER_COMMANDS change; all git + docker/systemctl rules stay raw $COMMAND because their danger token is a quotable argument of a non-DANGER command word; per-rule should-block + should-not-block tests; update 2 stale 'four' count comments).
requirement.what: Four scoped infra changes in .claude/ hooks + command docs + tests
requirement.why: Reduce context-strip false-positive surface (A), keep step-7 marker docs accurate (C/F), and confirm write-sentinel grant lifecycle parity (B)

included: conversion of EXACTLY two safe rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045, kill <PID> :1311) to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED
per-rule should-block + should-not-block tests for the two converted rules
verification of C and B parity with patch-on-gap
excluded: conversion of ANY git rule (reset --hard, push --force/--delete, checkout -- ., restore --source, revert <hash>, update-ref, branch -D, cherry-pick, rebase, stash push/save) — danger token is a quotable argument of non-DANGER 'git'

conversion of ANY docker/systemctl rule (docker compose down, docker happy, docker system prune -a, systemctl docker, systemctl verb) — danger token is a quotable argument of non-DANGER 'docker'/'systemctl'
ANY addition to DANGER_COMMANDS (pass1 'add git' step retracted)
any UI / app-runtime / user-triggered code path

success_criteria: 3-site marker parity verified (no surviving literal-task-id content predicate) or patched
write-sentinel candidate-key parity verified pretool<->posttool; CF-1 gated on existing-file overwrite
EXACTLY the two safe rules (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045, kill <PID> :1311) read COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; all git + docker/systemctl + path/value rules stay raw $COMMAND
each converted rule has should-block + should-not-block test with rule-specific payloads, both passing
no comment hardcodes 'four' as the rule count
constraints: fail-closed preserved (raw fallback on stripper error)

a rule is convertible ONLY if its match is identical raw-vs-stripped (command-word-anchored, OR argument-anchored where the command word is already a DANGER command); argument/option/subcommand-anchored rules on non-DANGER command words MUST stay raw (false-negative risk)
reuse existing COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED block; :1143 split-grep template not needed for the two convertible rules (no path predicate)

items.title: Test-folder cleanliness + commit.md:221 stale label
items.scope: Update the stale label at commands/commit.md:221 ('Mtime + literal-task-id glob') to describe the machine-readable continuation-marker grep predicate. mktemp/trap/755/naming dimensions verified ALREADY COMPLIANT: all 8 test_*.sh are mode 755; the 4 that create temp files (ac1, ac5, final_sweep, push_sentinel) use mktemp+trap EXIT INT TERM; the 4 read-only grep/awk tests (ac3, ac6, ac9, ac10) correctly omit them. push_sentinel_abort.sh:73 hardcoded /tmp path is the production sentinel dir under test, not a test temp file (intentional).
acceptance_criteria: commands/commit.md:221 no longer contains substring 'literal-task-id glob'
the corrected label describes the machine-readable continuation-marker grep, consistent with the body at commit.md:222

If dev finds a surviving literal-task-id content predicate anywhere in the step-7 path, patch it to the marker grep.

items.title: Write-guard sentinel three-candidate path (verify + harden)
items.scope: Verify candidate-key parity between pretool-write-guard.sh:79-85 and posttool-allowlist-consume.py:230-238 (both must enumerate [writer_key=CLAUDE_TASK_ID-else-session_id, data.task_id, session_id] de-duped in identical order). items.title: Extend executable-context stripping to remaining verb rules
items.scope: CORRECTED after QA pass1. Conversion criterion: a rule is safe to convert to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED ONLY IF its match result is IDENTICAL raw-vs-stripped for every command (conversion may remove false positives but must NEVER create a false negative). Verified stripper semantics (bash_context_strip.py:305-330): the command-word token (i<=cmd_idx) is preserved verbatim; for a NON-DANGER command word any QUOTED argument/option/subcommand/path token is ERASED (single->"", double->"<only $()/backtick>"); for a command word already in DANGER_COMMANDS quoted args are UNQUOTED (exposed). Therefore convert EXACTLY TWO previously-raw rules: (1) dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred (pretool-bash-safety.sh:1045) — command-word-anchored, signal preserved verbatim, removes false positives like echo "dd if=..."; (2) kill <PID> (pretool-bash-safety.sh:1311) — argument-anchored (kill\s+[0-9]) but kill is ALREADY in DANGER_COMMANDS so its args are exposed not erased (kill "1234" -> kill 1234), match identical raw-vs-stripped, mirrors the already-stripped kill -sig rule at :1119. Make NO DANGER_COMMANDS additions (the pass1 'add the command word to DANGER_COMMANDS' step is RETRACTED: for git rules the command word is 'git'; adding 'git' un-strips ALL git args globally per :305-314, re-introducing the quoted false positives the conversion was meant to remove and perturbing the shipped rm/mv interaction; neither convertible rule needs an entry). DO NOT convert any git rule (reset --hard :1405, push --force/--delete :1414, checkout <ref> -- . :1372, restore --source :1389, revert <hash>/ref-modifier :1461-1472, update-ref/branch -D :1421, cherry-pick/rebase :1441, stash push/save :1350-1361) — their danger token is a QUOTABLE ARGUMENT of the non-DANGER command word 'git' (git reset "--hard" executes a real hard reset but strips to 'git reset ' -> silent false negative). DO NOT convert any docker/systemctl rule (docker compose down|restart|stop :1076, docker stop|restart|rm|kill happy :1066, docker system prune -a :1083, systemctl restart|stop|disable docker :1052, systemctl <verb> non-happy :1129) — their subcommand/verb/target tokens are quotable ARGUMENTS of the non-DANGER command words 'docker'/'systemctl' (docker compose "down" is a real destructive down but strips to 'docker compose '). DO NOT convert any Class-2 path/value rule (.env/credentials/secret/password, /etc/docker/daemon.json, /root/happy/packages/, Dockerfile.webapp/HAPPY_SERVER_URL=, DB-mutation, session-API curl, sentinel-filename, session_dirs.txt, daemon-restart-flag, /usr/bin/happy, happy-daemon systemctl). Update the 2 stale count comments (pretool-bash-safety.sh:1089, bash_context_strip.py:5) that hardcode 'four'. Preserve fail-closed (raw fallback at :1096-1106). Highest-risk item.
files_to_modify: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh (convert ONLY the :1045 dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred rule and the :1311 kill <PID> rule to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED; update stale :1089 comment)
hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py (update stale :5 comment ONLY; DANGER_COMMANDS at :26 stays UNCHANGED — no new entry)
acceptance_criteria: EXACTLY two previously-raw rules converted to COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED: dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred (:1045) and kill <PID> (:1311)

NO git rule reads COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED (reset --hard :1405, push :1414, checkout :1372, restore :1389, revert :1461, update-ref/branch :1421, cherry-pick/rebase :1441, stash :1350-1361 all stay raw $COMMAND)
NO docker/systemctl rule converted (docker compose :1076, docker happy :1066, docker system prune -a :1083, systemctl docker :1052, systemctl verb :1129 stay raw)
NO Class-2 path/value rule converted
bash_context_strip.py:26 DANGER_COMMANDS is UNCHANGED ({killall,pkill,kill,rm,mv}); no new entry added
dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred should-block test: INPUT 'dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda' -> exit 2
dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred should-not-block test: INPUT 'echo "dd if=/dev/zero of=/dev/sda"' -> exit 0
kill <PID> should-block test: INPUT 'kill 1234' AND 'kill "1234"' both -> exit 2
kill <PID> should-not-block test: INPUT 'echo "kill 1234"' -> exit 0
AC-A1 is satisfied ONLY when both should-block AND should-not-block tests pass for both converted rules (textual COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED reference alone is insufficient evidence)
neither pretool-bash-safety.sh:1089 nor bash_context_strip.py:5 hardcodes 'four' as the rule count
fail-closed preserved: stripper error falls back to raw $COMMAND

ordering_rationale: F -> C -> B -> A. F first: smallest, zero-conflict (commit.md only), fast win, no dependency. C second: read-only verification that also touches commit.md, so colocating with F avoids re-reading the same file. B third: fully independent files (write-guard.sh / consume.py / allowlist.py), no overlap with F/C/A. A last and isolated: A is the only high-regression-risk item and the sole writer of pretool-bash-safety.sh + bash_context_strip.py + new tests. B and A are SEQUENTIAL (not parallel) because B touches hooks/lib/allowlist.py and A's new tests import from hooks.lib; sequencing avoids shared-file/import churn and keeps the high-risk A change reviewable in isolation. Land A only after F/C/B are green.

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Backlog of 4 partially-implemented or cleanliness items; A has a coverage gap (only 4 of ~40 grep rules use context-stripping); F/C have stale documentation; B/C need parity verification.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Item A: the context-strip infrastructure (bash_context_strip.py) and the COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED setup were built for the 4 original danger-verb rules (kill/pkill/rm/mv); the remaining verb rules were never migrated, and two comments hardcode the count 'four'. Items C/F: the step-7 predicate was migrated to a machine-readable marker but the commit.md:221 section LABEL still says 'literal-task-id glob'. Items B/C are already-correct implementations needing audit confirmation.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A - multi-item backlog, not a single regression
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: Incremental hardening: context-stripping (A) and the marker predicate (C) were introduced for specific rules/sites and not back-filled across all analogous sites; labels/comments lagged the implementation.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: A: raw-$COMMAND verb rules false-positive on quoted-string mentions of danger verbs. F/C: stale label misleads future readers about the step-7 predicate. B: unverified candidate-key parity could leak or fail-to-consume grants.
observations: 4 rules use COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED at pretool-bash-safety.sh:1110,1119,1143,1154 (inferred: source read)
~36 rules grep raw $COMMAND, enumerated by grep of 'echo "$COMMAND" | grep' (inferred: source read)
stale 'four' comments at pretool-bash-safety.sh:1089 and bash_context_strip.py:5 (inferred: grep)
marker is sole content predicate: step7-spec-update.py:134 marker def, :148 'if marker in content' (inferred: source read)
commit.md:222 body greps '<!-- spec-continuation-of: ${TASK_ID} -->' as ONLY content predicate; :221 label says 'literal-task-id glob' (inferred: source read)
CF-1 suppression gated on '[ -f "$FILE_PATH" ]' at write-guard.sh:114 (inferred: source read)
write-guard candidate order [writer_key,data_task_id,session_id] at :79-85; consume order [writer_primary,data_task_id,session_id] at :235 (inferred: source read)
all 8 test_*.sh mode 755; ac1/ac5/final_sweep/push_sentinel use mktemp+trap; ac3/ac6/ac9/ac10 are read-only no-temp tests (observed: stat + grep)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Each cited file:line was read directly this session; grep enumerated the raw-vs-stripped rule split and the stale count comments. No runtime reproduction required — this is an analysis/spec-only cycle on static infra files.
root_cause_analysis.located_source: hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1089-1472 (rule set), hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py:26 (DANGER_COMMANDS), commands/commit.md:221-222, hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:60-116, hooks/posttool-allowlist-consume.py:222-272, hooks/lib/allowlist.py:523-576, scripts/step7-spec-update.py:134-148

pre_existing_guards.purpose: fail-closed: on stripper error/timeout, COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED stays equal to raw $COMMAND so no block is silently dropped
CF-1: suppress legacy grant ONLY on overwrite of existing file; new-file creation must not be blocked by an unrelated sentinel
split-grep template: stripped view for verb, raw view for path so quoted paths still match

setup.reason: non-UI -- hook / config / agent-prompt edit; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. All targets are .claude/ infra (shell hooks, a Python lib, markdown command docs, shell test scripts).

evidence.observed: Prior Opus 4.8 review: A has only 4 of ~40 rules stripped; C marker aligned; B three paths exist; F naming compliant + 2 stale comments + commit.md:221 label.

measured.value: 4 stripped rules (1110,1119,1143,1154) vs ~36 raw-$COMMAND rules; DANGER_COMMANDS={killall,pkill,kill,rm,mv}; marker is sole step-7 predicate; CF-1 gated on -f FILE_PATH; commit.md:221 label='Mtime + literal-task-id glob'; 2 'four' comments at bash-safety:1089 & strip.py:5

expected.value: ONLY the two raw-vs-stripped-identical rules (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045 command-word-anchored, kill <PID> :1311 kill-is-DANGER) match on the stripped view; all git + docker/systemctl + path/value rules stay raw (their danger token is a quotable argument of a non-DANGER command word -> stripping would create false negatives); DANGER_COMMANDS unchanged; marker is sole predicate (already true); CF-1 overwrite-only (already true); label describes marker grep; comments do not hardcode 'four'
expected.source: this BA spec (pass2) + verified stripper semantics at bash_context_strip.py:305-330 (tier_2_verified)

evidence.gap: A: ONLY 2 convertible rules (dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred :1045, kill <PID> :1311) + 2 stale 'four' comments. (pass1 over-estimated ~12 convertible rules; the git and docker/systemctl rules are argument-anchored on non-DANGER command words and are NOT convertible.) F: 1 stale label. C/B: no functional gap found (verification-confirm expected).

reference_source.description: Live source verified by BA this session; existing :1143/:1154 split-grep pattern and :1094-1107 COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED setup are the copy templates

role_table._source: no project-root CLAUDE.md role table applicable to this infra cycle (token roles are for UI design systems)

novelty_check.rationale: No prior attempt on this backlog batch; first cycle.

development_approach.strategy: Execute in order F -> C -> B -> A. F and C are documentation-parity changes on commit.md (+read-only verification of spec-update.md/step7-spec-update.py). B is read-and-confirm parity across write-guard.sh/consume.py/allowlist.py, patching only on divergence. A is the substantive change: convert ONLY the two rules whose match is identical raw-vs-stripped -- dd|mkfs|fdisk|shred (:1045, command-word-anchored) and kill <PID> (:1311, argument-anchored but kill is already in DANGER_COMMANDS so its args are exposed not erased) -- to read COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. Make NO DANGER_COMMANDS change. Leave every git rule and every docker/systemctl rule as raw $COMMAND because their danger token is a quotable argument/subcommand of a non-DANGER command word, which the stripper erases when quoted (e.g. git reset "--hard" -> 'git reset ' -> silent false negative). Add per-rule should-block + should-not-block tests with rule-specific payloads driving the hook end-to-end via piped INPUT JSON (4 cases total + a recommended regression guard that a real git reset --hard still blocks via the unchanged raw rule); update the 2 stale 'four' comments; preserve the fail-closed raw fallback.
files_to_create: hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context_rules.py (or .sh) — per-rule should-block + should-not-block end-to-end tests for Item A
development_approach.validation_approach: Item A: run the new should-block (verb in executable context -> exit 2) and should-not-block (verb only in quoted string/heredoc -> exit 0) tests per converted rule, plus the existing test_bash_safety_context.py suite for stripper regressions. Items F/C: grep assertions that commit.md:221 no longer contains 'literal-task-id glob' and that the marker is the sole step-7 predicate. Item B: assert candidate-key order parity and CF-1 -f gating by source inspection. Frozen spec untouched (git diff == 0).
